desires: complete the trivial chore before bed to secure a visible win without risking academic failure
finish the term project draft by Thursday so I can avoid any future conflict or anxiety about the deadline
maintain my reputation as reliable and high-performing within the cohort_3 group despite feeling exhausted

goals.description: Execute the trivial chore by 22:00 to secure an immediate, low-friction win that resets my cognitive baseline before tackling the term project.
goals.rationale: This aligns with my primary driver: academic outcomes. The 'instant win' provides a psychological buffer against my current exhaustion without requiring social negotiation or admitting weakness. Once this is checked off, I must prioritize the term project to avoid the cognitive dissonance of failing the 'completed' status marker by Thursday. Delaying risks perception of unreliability to both professor and teammates.

goals.description: Maintain strict invisibility regarding my schedule; do not engage in deep conversation with cohort_3 about my fatigue or lack of sleep.
goals.rationale: Interpersonal friction is a high cost to my limited energy reserves. While I trust the group, discussing my failure to rest would feel like an admission of defeat in my own discipline. I will passively avoid visibility on my plans to preserve cohesion while protecting my bandwidth for when it actually matters.

agent.last_reasoning: Securing the 'instant win' resets my cognitive baseline before tackling the term project. This avoids the friction of admitting failure to myself or others, preserving my discipline image while minimizing social overhead.
